title: Federal Judge Restricts ICE Less-Lethal Weapons at Portland ICE Facility
excerpt: U.S. District Judge Michael H. Simon issued a temporary restraining order barring ICE agents at Portland's ICE facility from deploying tear gas, pepper balls, and other less-lethal munitions against protesters unless facing imminent physical harm, citing repeated violations of constitutional rights.
content:
On February 3, 2026, U.S. District Judge Michael H. Simon issued a 14-day temporary restraining order barring federal agents at Portland's ICE building from deploying kinetic impact projectiles, pepper ball guns, pepper spray, tear gas, flashbang grenades, and other less-lethal munitions unless agents faced an imminent threat of physical harm.

The order came after federal agents on January 31, 2026 deployed massive amounts of chemical agents against a peaceful union-organized march that included children, seniors, and an 83-year-old Vietnam War veteran and his 84-year-old wife. Judge Simon wrote that "the repeated shooting and teargassing of nonviolent protesters at the Portland ICE Building will likely keep recurring," and that "statements made by DHS officials and senior federal executives show that the culture of the agency and its employees is to celebrate violent responses over fair and diplomatic ones." Simon noted the nation was "at a crossroads" between constitutional democracy and authoritarianism.

  • The restraining order was part of Dickinson v. Trump, a case brought by ACLU Oregon on behalf of protesters and freelance journalists beginning in November 2025
  • On March 9, 2026, Judge Simon converted the restraining order into a preliminary injunction and granted the plaintiffs class-action status, covering all nonviolent protesters and journalists at the facility
  • On March 25, 2026, the 9th Circuit Court of Appeals paused both the Simon injunction and a related order in a 2-1 split decision; oral arguments were scheduled for April 7, 2026
mediaPlaceholder: Judge Simon's 22-page restraining order and photographs of federal agents deploying tear gas against the Portland crowd
